text.title: mq-patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for mq-patterns as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using request/reply queues for latency-sensitive operations under 50 ms, such as authentication token validation or read-heavy API lookups.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Queue round-trip adds broker serialization latency on top of network latency. For sub-50 ms requirements, direct synchronous gRPC or HTTP is the correct choice.

failure-mode.description: Fan-out without per-consumer queues — broadcasting to a single shared queue that all consumers read from.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Competing consumers on a shared queue produce work-queue semantics, not fan-out. Each consumer gets only a fraction of messages. Use a broker-level exchange (RabbitMQ topic/fanout) or SNS topic to create one queue per consumer group.

failure-mode.description: Missing correlation IDs — publishing messages without a stable trace identifier across services.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without a correlation ID, a message cannot be traced through the system when it fails. Debugging becomes a log-grepping exercise across multiple services with no join key.
